questions.question: Which of the following are PFC subdivisions discussed in the lecture? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: DLPFC, VLPFC, vmPFC/OFC, dmPFC/ACC, and frontopolar cortex are all PFC subdivisions covered in the lecture. V1 is primary visual cortex in the occipital lobe and is not part of the PFC.

questions.question: Which regions form the wider executive function network beyond the PFC? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Executive function arises from a distributed network: posterior parietal cortex (LIP encodes expected value), basal ganglia, mediodorsal thalamus (the relay), and ACC. There is no single homunculus — the network is the unit of analysis.

questions.question: Which are the four forms of inhibition listed in the book? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Halting behaviours that are well-trained or previously valid
options.B: Preventing irrelevant information from interfering with ongoing processing
options.D: Removing irrelevant information from working memory

questions.explanation: The four forms of inhibition are halting well-trained behaviours, preventing interference, restraining socially inappropriate behaviour, and clearing irrelevant items from working memory. Encoding semantic memories is not an inhibition function.

questions.question: Which statements about Phineas Gage are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: A tamping iron drove through his orbitofrontal and medial PFC
options.B: He is the classic case of disinhibition syndrome
options.C: The traditional 'reckless and profane' narrative is now considered exaggerated
options.D: He stayed employed for years and travelled to South America
options.E: He had bilateral medial temporal lobe removal

questions.explanation: Gage's tamping iron damaged orbitofrontal and medial PFC, making him the classic disinhibition case. The lecture noted the 'transformed personality' narrative is exaggerated — he stayed employed and travelled. He did not have MTL removal; that was patient H.M.

questions.question: Which findings come from Wallis et al. (2001)? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Single-unit recordings in lateral PFC of monkeys
options.B: Neurons selective for the rule itself, independent of cue (juice vs tone)
options.C: Neurons selective independent of the specific stimuli (Object A vs B)
options.D: Used a matching / non-matching task with cues signalling the rule
options.E: Demonstrated that the hippocampus encodes abstract rules

questions.explanation: Wallis et al. (2001) recorded single units in monkey lateral PFC during a matching/non-matching task and found rule-selective neurons whose firing was independent of the cue used and the specific stimuli. The hippocampus was not the focus — the PFC encodes abstract rules.

questions.question: Which features describe the frontal dysexecutive syndrome (lateral PFC damage)? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Don't start anything new; can't plan into the future
options.C: Constant unproductive movement and abnormal humor
options.D: Confabulate implausible explanations for life problems
options.E: Impaired on response selection and working memory tasks

questions.explanation: Dysexecutive syndrome (lateral PFC) features failure to initiate, apathy, confabulation, and impaired response selection / working memory. Constant unproductive movement and abnormal humor characterise the disinhibition syndrome (ventromedial PFC).

questions.question: Which statements about the Stroop task are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Congruent: word 'BLUE' printed in blue ink — no conflict
options.B: Incongruent: word 'YELLOW' printed in green ink — strong conflict
options.C: Neutral: a non-colour word like 'TRUCK' printed in red — partial control
options.D: ACC activates more on incongruent trials
options.E: High ACC activity on trial N predicts lower DLPFC activity on trial N+1

questions.explanation: The Stroop task contrasts congruent, incongruent, and neutral trials, with ACC showing greater activation on incongruent trials. High ACC activity on trial N actually predicts HIGHER (not lower) DLPFC activity on trial N+1, reflecting downstream control adjustment.

questions.question: Which statements correctly describe the ERN (error-related negativity)? (Select all that apply)

options.A: A negative-polarity scalp potential measured with EEG
options.C: Has both response-ERN and feedback-ERN variants
options.D: Amplitude correlates with slower response time on the next trial
options.E: Is generated primarily in the cerebellum

questions.explanation: The ERN is a negative scalp potential, originally localised to ACC, with response and feedback variants. Larger ERN predicts slower next-trial RT, reflecting engagement of control. It is not generated in the cerebellum.

questions.question: Which are points of agreement OR disagreement between the Baddeley and Cowan working memory models? (Select all that apply)

options.B: Baddeley uses modality-specific buffers; Cowan does not
options.C: Cowan treats working memory as activated long-term memory illuminated by an attentional spotlight
options.D: Baddeley includes a phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, and episodic buffer
options.E: Both deny that working memory is capacity-limited

questions.explanation: Both models include a central executive — that consensus is the takeaway. Baddeley uses modular buffers (phonological loop, sketchpad, episodic buffer); Cowan posits unitary activated LTM with an attentional spotlight. Both agree working memory IS capacity-limited.

questions.question: Which findings come from the cutting-edge 7T fMRI meditation paper? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Reduced DMN activity during meditation versus passive rest
options.B: Posterior cingulate cortex and medial PFC are core DMN nodes that deactivate
options.C: Thalamic nuclei and occipital cortex also deactivate, suggesting perceptual decoupling
options.D: Effects survive after controlling for alertness and effort covariates
options.E: Multiple regions showed strong increases in activity vs rest

questions.explanation: The 7T study found DMN deactivation (PCC + mPFC core nodes), thalamic and occipital deactivation suggesting perceptual decoupling, and effects robust to covariate control. Critically, NO brain region showed increased activity vs rest.
